title: arenaria arvensis
intro: Arenaria arvensis, also known as Sandwort or Chamomile of the Field, is a low-growing, annual or biennial herb that belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which can help soothe digestive issues and calm nervous disorders. The health benefits of Arenaria arvensis include reducing anxiety and stress, promoting a good night's sleep, and alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Arenaria arvensis can be prepared as an herbal tea by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water, or as a tincture by mixing the plant extracts with a carrier oil or liquid, offering a convenient way to harness its medicinal properties.
